content_markdown: A DC electric motor converts $75\%$ of the input electrical energy to mechanical energy. The remaining $25\%$ is

options.label: C
options.text: used to maintain the potential difference of the battery.

vibe_explanation: In any real-world energy conversion process, efficiency is less than 100% because some energy is always lost to the surroundings. In an electric motor, this 'lost' energy is primarily due to resistance in the coils (Joule heating) and friction in the bearings, both of which result in the dissipation of thermal energy.
